  • Constructor Details

    • MailSendRecvJpaRepositoryImpl

      public MailSendRecvJpaRepositoryImpl()

  • Method Details

    • getMailsByUserOrRole

      public MailInboxWithSend getMailsByUserOrRole()
      Description copied from interface: MailSendRecvJpaRepositoryCustom
      Retrieves all mail messages for the currently authenticated user, including both direct messages and role-based messages.

      This method returns messages that are:

      • Directly addressed to or from the user
      • Sent to roles that the user belongs to
      • Not marked as hidden or deleted by the user

      The returned object also includes counts of role-based conversation replies to help with UI display and notification management.

      Specified by:
      getMailsByUserOrRole in interface MailSendRecvJpaRepositoryCustom
      Returns:
      a MailInboxWithSend containing the user's mail messages and conversation reply counts

    • markForRead

      public MailSendRecv markForRead(Integer idMailSendRecv)
      Description copied from interface: MailSendRecvJpaRepositoryCustom
      Marks a specific mail message as read by the currently authenticated user.

      This method updates the read status in the mail_send_recv_read_del table and returns the message with updated read status information for immediate use in the UI.

      Specified by:
      markForRead in interface MailSendRecvJpaRepositoryCustom
      Parameters:
      idMailSendRecv - the unique identifier of the mail message to mark as read
      Returns:
      the MailSendRecv entity with updated read status and role name information attached

    • hideDeleteResource

      public void hideDeleteResource(Integer idMailSendRecv)
      Description copied from interface: MailSendRecvJpaRepositoryCustom
      Hides or deletes a mail message or conversation thread for the currently authenticated user.

      The behavior depends on the message type and user ownership:

      • One-to-one messages: Physically deleted from the database if the user is the sender or receiver
      • Role-based messages: Marked as hidden/deleted in the mail_send_recv_read_del table (soft delete)
      • Conversation threads: All related messages in the thread are processed according to the above rules

      This method ensures users can only hide/delete messages they have access to, providing appropriate security checks.

      Specified by:
      hideDeleteResource in interface MailSendRecvJpaRepositoryCustom
      Parameters:
      idMailSendRecv - the unique identifier of the mail message or conversation thread to hide/delete

    • saveOnlyAttributes

      public MailSendRecv saveOnlyAttributes(MailSendRecv mailSendRecv, MailSendRecv existingMailSendRecv, Set<Class<? extends Annotation>> updatePropertyLevelClasses) throws Exception
      This message comes in via the REST API and needs additional validation.
      Specified by:
      saveOnlyAttributes in interface BaseRepositoryCustom<MailSendRecv>
      Parameters:
      mailSendRecv - the entity containing the new values to be saved
      existingMailSendRecv - the current entity from the database, null for new entities
      updatePropertyLevelClasses - set of annotation classes that define update permission levels
      Returns:
      the saved entity with updated attributes
      Throws:
      Exception - if the save operation fails, validation errors occur, or security constraints are violated

    • getUpdatePropertyLevels

      public Set<Class<? extends Annotation>> getUpdatePropertyLevels(MailSendRecv existingEntity)
      Description copied from interface: BaseRepositoryCustom
      Determines the property-level update permissions for the current user context. Analyzes user roles and entity state to return applicable annotation classes for field-level access control.
      Specified by:
      getUpdatePropertyLevels in interface BaseRepositoryCustom<MailSendRecv>
      Parameters:
      existingEntity - the existing entity to determine update permissions for
      Returns:
      set of annotation classes representing the user's update permission levels
